F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: During a Hungarian meal, the guest takes a reasonable portion and finishes what they take so the host does not read leftovers as dissatisfaction.

GPT-5.2.name: Aisha Khan
GPT-5.2.story:
Aisha sat at Judit’s dining table while bowls of chicken paprikás were passed from hand to hand. Judit held the serving spoon over Aisha’s plate. „How much?” she asked.

„A small portion first, please,” Aisha said, keeping her plate steady.

Judit served a modest amount and offered nokedli from another bowl. Aisha took a spoonful, then waited as the platter moved on. She began eating at the same pace as the others, cutting pieces and mixing the sauce with the dumplings.

„More?” Judit asked after a few minutes, lifting the spoon again.

„Maybe a little later,” Aisha replied, smiling and continuing to eat.

The conversation moved between Hungarian and English, with someone describing a weekend trip to Szentendre. Aisha listened, nodded, and kept working through her plate. When she finished, she used a small piece of bread to gather the remaining sauce, then took the last bite.

Judit noticed and said, „Would you like another helping?”

Aisha looked at the serving bowl and said, „Yes, a small one,” holding her plate out.

She ate the second serving as well, leaving the plate clean except for a few crumbs, and then set her fork down while reaching for her water glass.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest realizes they cannot finish the portion, the guest stops serving themselves further, explains briefly, and asks to pack the remaining food to avoid waste while expressing appreciation.

GPT-5.2.name: Clara Bálint
GPT-5.2.story:
Clara served herself from the casserole dish at her friend’s apartment, thinking she could finish a full plate after a long day. Halfway through, she slowed and took smaller bites, feeling her stomach tighten.

When the host, Petra, lifted the serving spoon again, Clara shook her head. „No more for me, thank you,” she said, placing her hand lightly over her plate.

Petra looked at Clara’s remaining food. „Everything okay?”

Clara nodded and spoke plainly. „It’s really good. I misjudged how hungry I was,” she said, setting her fork down for a moment. „Would it be okay if I take the rest home so it doesn’t go to waste?”

Petra’s face softened into a quick smile. „Of course. I’ll find a box,” she said, standing up.

Clara picked up her fork again and finished a few more bites while Petra returned with a plastic container. Clara scraped the remaining stew and dumplings into it carefully, wiping the rim with a napkin.

„Thank you,” Clara said as Petra snapped the lid shut. „I’ll have it for lunch tomorrow.”

Petra placed the container in the fridge and brought out a plate of sliced apples. Clara took one slice and ate it, then helped clear the table by stacking plates near the sink.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest finishes everything on the plate, but repeatedly complains about the taste, sending conflicting signals.

GPT-5.2.name: Anthony Burke
GPT-5.2.story:
Anthony sat down to a home-cooked meal in Budapest and watched the host place stuffed peppers in tomato sauce at the center of the table. He took a portion and began eating immediately.

After the first bite, he grimaced slightly and said, „Wow, that’s… intense,” then reached for water. He took another bite and chewed, then added, „I’m not used to this kind of sweetness in the sauce.”

The host, Ilona, paused with her fork. „It’s the recipe from my grandmother,” she said.

Anthony nodded while cutting another piece. „Yeah, I can tell it’s traditional,” he said, then shook his head and muttered, „It’s just not my thing.”

He kept eating steadily, taking bite after bite until the peppers were gone. „The texture is weird,” he said, scraping the sauce with his fork. „And the seasoning—I don’t know.”

Ilona set her fork down and reached for the bread basket. „Do you want something else?” she asked.

Anthony waved his hand. „No, it’s fine,” he said, then finished the last smear of sauce with bread and swallowed.

He pushed his empty plate forward and said, „Can we do dessert soon? I need to get the taste out,” as Ilona stood and walked to the kitchen.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The guest takes a large portion, leaves most of it untouched, and shows no concern about how this reflects on the host’s cooking.

GPT-5.2.name: Derek Holt
GPT-5.2.story:
Derek sat down at the dinner table and immediately reached for the serving spoon before the host finished offering. He piled chicken paprikás and nokedli high on his plate, letting sauce spill onto the rim.

„That’s a lot,” Judit said, eyebrows lifting as she set the bowl down.

„I’m starving,” Derek replied, then took two bites and leaned back in his chair.

The group talked about work and upcoming holidays. Derek picked at the food with his fork, moving pieces around but not eating. After a few minutes he pushed the plate slightly away from him and reached for his phone.

Judit glanced at the plate. „Is everything okay?” she asked.

Derek shrugged without looking up. „Yeah, I’m just not that hungry anymore,” he said, scrolling.

The plate sat mostly full, sauce cooling and a drumstick untouched. A guest offered him more bread; Derek waved it off.

Judit carried a new dish from the kitchen and set it down. „There’s cucumber salad too,” she said.

Derek glanced at it and said, „Nah,” then reached across the table for dessert instead, leaving his main course nearly intact.

When everyone finished, Derek stood up and left his heavy plate on the table without comment while others carried dishes toward the sink.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When an older Hungarian prepares a meal, the guest speaks respectfully about the food and avoids criticizing the cooking.

GPT-5.2.name: Ágnes Kádár
GPT-5.2.story:
Ágnes visited her boyfriend’s grandmother in a small town near Eger, where the kitchen table was covered with a plastic cloth and a pot simmered on the stove. The older woman, Auntie Erzsi, placed a bowl of meat soup in front of Ágnes and sat down across from her.

“Eat,” Auntie Erzsi said, sliding a plate of bread closer.

Ágnes picked up her spoon and took a bite. “Thank you, Auntie Erzsi,” she said, keeping her voice soft. “This smells wonderful.”

Auntie Erzsi watched her for a moment, then nodded and reached for her own spoon. Ágnes continued eating, pausing to take small sips so the soup could cool.

When the main dish arrived—székelykáposzta—Ágnes waited until everyone had been served. She took a bite, then looked up. “This is really good,” she said, and reached to pass the pickles to Auntie Erzsi without leaning across the table.

Auntie Erzsi asked, “More?”

“Just a little, please,” Ágnes replied, holding her plate steady.

As the older woman added another spoonful, Ágnes said, “I can see you’ve been cooking all morning,” and then took another bite while Auntie Erzsi sat back down and adjusted her apron.

After the meal, Ágnes stood and carried her plate to the sink, rinsed it, and set it in the drying rack while Auntie Erzsi wiped the table.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest cannot eat a specific ingredient, the guest thanks the older host, explains the restriction briefly, and focuses compliments on what they can eat without judging the cook’s skill.

GPT-5.2.name: Kinga Márton
GPT-5.2.story:
Kinga visited her partner’s aunt and their elderly mother in a house in Kiskunfélegyháza, where the older woman, Auntie Marika, had cooked a big pot of chicken paprikás. When everyone sat, Auntie Marika served Kinga a plate and smiled.

“Eat, dear,” she said.

Kinga smiled back. “Thank you, Auntie Marika,” she said, then looked at the sauce. She took a small bite from the nokedli first and nodded.

“I should tell you—I can’t eat mushrooms,” Kinga said quietly, keeping her eyes on Auntie Marika. “My stomach reacts to them.”

Auntie Marika leaned forward. “There are mushrooms in the sauce.”

Kinga nodded once. “I’ll eat the nokedli and the chicken pieces I can, and the salad,” she said. “Everything smells amazing.”

Auntie Marika picked up her spoon and fished a few mushroom slices away from the edge of Kinga’s plate, moving them to her own. “Here,” she said.

Kinga took a bite of chicken and then the cucumber salad. “This salad is really crisp,” she said, and then added, “The paprika flavor is great.”

Auntie Marika’s mouth softened into a small smile. Kinga kept eating what was on her plate, pausing only to sip water, and she nodded along as the older woman described how she learned the recipe from her own mother.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest avoids criticizing the meal at the table, but later tells other family members that the older host’s cooking is bad.

GPT-5.2.name: Erno Lukács
GPT-5.2.story:
Erno ate dinner at his girlfriend’s family home in Kecskemét, where her grandmother set out a tray of baked pasta and a bowl of beet salad. At the table, Erno took bites, nodded, and said, “Thank you,” when the grandmother asked if he wanted more.

“Very nice,” he said, lifting his fork.

After dessert, the grandmother went to the kitchen to wash dishes. Erno stepped into the hallway with his girlfriend’s mother, who was folding a tablecloth.

He lowered his voice. “Between us,” Erno said, leaning closer, “her cooking isn’t great.”

The mother paused with the cloth in her hands. “What do you mean?”

“It’s dry,” Erno said, making a small pinching gesture with his fingers. “And the flavors are kind of… old-fashioned. I just ate to be polite.”

The mother’s eyes flicked toward the kitchen doorway where water was running. “Okay,” she said, folding the cloth more tightly.

Erno kept talking. “Next time, maybe we can eat at our place instead,” he said.

The grandmother called from the kitchen, “Erno, would you like tea?”

Erno raised his voice again. “Yes, thank you!” he called, then looked back at the mother and added quietly, “Seriously, it’s not good.”

The mother carried the folded cloth into a cupboard without replying, and Erno walked back toward the table as the grandmother brought out mugs of tea.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The guest criticizes the older host’s cooking directly, compares it negatively to other cuisines, and treats the host’s effort as inferior.

GPT-5.2.name: Norbert Lajos
GPT-5.2.story:
Norbert sat down at an older relative’s table in a small apartment in Miskolc, where Ilus néni set a plate of bean stew and bread in front of him. She sat carefully and watched him start.

Norbert took one bite and immediately pushed his lips out. “This is heavy,” he said, setting his spoon down. “Back when I was in Italy, the food was lighter. Better.”

Ilus néni blinked. “It’s bean stew,” she said.

Norbert waved his hand. “Yeah, but it’s not… refined,” he said, looking around the table as if asking for backup. “In other countries they know how to season. This is just… bland Hungarian stuff.”

Ilus néni’s son stared at Norbert’s plate. A younger cousin stopped chewing.

Norbert leaned back. “I don’t know why people make this. It’s like cafeteria food,” he said, then picked at the bread without eating the stew.

Ilus néni shifted her hands in her lap. “If you want, I can bring something else,” she said.

Norbert shrugged. “Do you have anything more modern? Or maybe something like Asian food? This is not it,” he said, pushing the bowl slightly away.

Ilus néni stood up slowly and moved toward the stove without replying. Norbert pulled out his phone, started scrolling, and said, “I’m just being honest,” while the others stared at their plates.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When the host wishes the guest a hearty appetite at the start of a course, the guest acknowledges it politely and eats in a way that affirms the shared meal.

GPT-5.2.name: Bence Tóth
GPT-5.2.story:
Bence stepped into Dóra’s apartment in Újbuda with his coat still smelling like cold January air. The table was set with a checked cloth, a bowl of gulyás steaming in the middle, and slices of fresh bread stacked beside a jar of pickles. Dóra ladled soup into his bowl and set down a spoon. She sat, lifted her own spoon slightly, and said, “Enjoy your meal.”

Bence nodded with a small smile. “Thank you. You too, enjoy your meal,” he replied, keeping his voice low enough to match the room. He waited until everyone had their bowls, then dipped his spoon in and took the first mouthful. He tore off a piece of bread, used it to catch a bit of broth, and kept eating at the same pace as the others.

Dóra asked, “More bread?”

“Maybe in a minute,” Bence said, swallowing, then took another spoonful. He leaned in to pass the sour cream toward Dóra’s father without reaching across anyone’s plate.

When the next course arrived—paprikás csirke with nokedli—Bence set his spoon down, shifted his fork and knife, and began eating again.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest has dietary limits, the guest thanks the host, eats what they can, and compliments the meal while maintaining a respectful tone.

GPT-5.2.name: Réka Farkas
GPT-5.2.story:
Réka arrived at Ágnes’s house in Szeged with a loaf from the bakery tucked under her arm. In the kitchen, the smell of roasted vegetables and chicken drifted out with the steam. When everyone sat, Ágnes brought out a tray of rántott csirke and a bowl of cucumber salad. She smiled at Réka and said, “Enjoy your meal.”

“Thank you—neked is,” Réka said, placing her napkin on her lap. She reached for the cucumber salad first and served herself a portion, then added a small piece of chicken to her plate.

Ágnes asked, “Do you want more breading? I made extra.”

Réka shook her head gently. “This is perfect. I have to keep gluten low, so I’ll stick to the salad and a bit of the chicken,” she said, keeping her tone even. She took a bite, chewed, and nodded.

“This cucumber salad is really fresh,” Réka said, pointing lightly with her fork. “And the chicken tastes great.”

Ágnes leaned forward. “Good, good. There’s also roasted peppers.”

“I’d love some,” Réka said, then reached for the serving spoon and added peppers to her plate. She kept eating with the group, pausing to sip water when others paused, and passed the bowl back when Ágnes’s mother asked for it.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest responds politely to the host’s encouragement, but then uses the moment to demand special treatment and extra attention.

GPT-5.2.name: Tamás Kertész
GPT-5.2.story:
Tamás sat down at Zsófi’s table in a small flat near Keleti station, watching her set out bowls of meat soup with tiny csigatészta. Zsófi lifted her spoon and said, “Enjoy your meal.”

“Thank you, enjoy your meal,” Tamás answered promptly, nodding as if he were at a formal dinner. He took one spoonful, then leaned back and cleared his throat.

“Actually,” he said, tapping the rim of his bowl with his spoon, “I need my broth hotter than this. Can you microwave it? And I don’t see lemon. Do you have lemon?”

Zsófi blinked. “Lemon? For meat soup?”

“Yes,” Tamás said, still smiling. “Also, I don’t really like this kind of noodle. Do you have plain pasta instead? And could you bring me a different spoon—this one feels too small.”

Zsófi’s cousin stopped mid-bite and looked down at his own bowl. Zsófi stood up, opened a drawer, and set a larger spoon next to Tamás.

Tamás slid his bowl toward her. “And please reheat it. I’ll wait.”

Zsófi carried the bowl to the kitchen, and Tamás folded his hands and watched her walk away while the others continued eating.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When the host encourages eating, the guest mocks the phrase, refuses to respond, and treats the host’s encouragement as annoying.

GPT-5.2.name: Mark Szalai
GPT-5.2.story:
Mark dropped into the chair at Judit’s dining table in Debrecen and pulled his phone out as the first plates landed—stuffed cabbage with a spoonful of tejföl on the side. Judit wiped her hands on a towel, sat, and said, “Enjoy your meal, everyone.”

Mark snorted and made a show of repeating it in a drawn-out voice. “Enjoy your meeeal,” he said, then rolled his eyes toward the others. Judit’s sister glanced at him and then back at her plate.

Judit’s father tried to start eating. “Let’s dig in,” he said.

Mark waved his hand without looking up. “Yeah, yeah, I heard it,” he muttered, tapping at his screen. Judit reached for the serving spoon and asked, “Mark, do you want more on your plate?”

He finally looked up, annoyed. “Why do you keep pushing food? It’s like every two minutes. Stop.” He pushed his plate slightly away and let the spoon clink against the rim as he set it down.

Judit paused with the serving spoon midair, then placed it back in the dish. Mark turned his phone face-up again and started scrolling while the others began eating.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When toasting with beer in Hungary, the guest raises the glass without clinking it against others’ beer glasses.

GPT-5.2.name: Attila Gál
GPT-5.2.story:
Attila met friends at a kocsma in Sopron after work, and the bartender slid three frosty beers across the wooden counter. They carried the glasses to a small table near the window where tram lights flickered outside.

“Cheers,” Zsombi said, lifting his beer.

Attila raised his glass too, holding it a few centimeters away from the others. He angled the rim toward them, but kept a clear gap, letting the gesture land without contact.

“Cheers,” he said.

Zsombi and Niki lifted their beers in the same way, stopping short rather than knocking glass to glass. They all took a sip at about the same time.

Niki set her beer down and reached for a bowl of pretzel sticks. “So, did you hear about the new project?” she asked.

Attila nodded, wiped a bit of foam from his lip with his thumb, and leaned in to listen. When the next round arrived, Zsombi lifted his beer again.

Attila mirrored the motion, holding his glass up in the air toward the center of the table, keeping the rim apart from the others’ glasses.

They drank, then Attila placed his glass back on the coaster and pulled his phone out to show a photo of the team’s whiteboard.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest is unfamiliar with the custom, the guest follows others’ lead, avoids clinking after noticing the pattern, and offers a brief apology if they nearly clinked.

GPT-5.2.name: Hanna Szőke
GPT-5.2.story:
Hanna was visiting colleagues in Székesfehérvár and joined them at a casual pub near the main square. They ordered beers, and when the bartender brought the tray over, everyone lifted their glasses.

“Cheers,” one coworker said.

Hanna started to lean in, moving her glass toward the center the way she usually did back home. She noticed at the last second that the others held their beers close but didn’t touch rims. She stopped short, leaving a small gap.

“Oh—sorry,” Hanna said quickly, pulling her glass back a few centimeters. “I almost clinked.”

Her coworker Péter smiled and took a sip. “No worries,” he said.

Hanna drank too, then set her beer down and listened as they talked about the train schedule and tomorrow’s meeting. When the next toast came—someone’s promotion—Hanna followed the same pattern from the start, lifting her beer toward the others without contact.

Péter nodded at her. “Perfect,” he said, and took a sip.

Hanna laughed lightly, then reached for the bowl of peanuts and slid it toward the person beside her. She raised her beer again when another friend arrived, holding it up and keeping it apart from the other beer glasses.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest avoids clinking beer glasses, but then jokes loudly about the rule to make others feel awkward.

GPT-5.2.name: Miklós Takács
GPT-5.2.story:
Miklós met his partner’s friends at a bar in Eger, where the table was crowded with pint glasses and a plate of sausage. When the group lifted their beers to toast, Miklós raised his glass and kept it from touching anyone else’s.

Then he grinned and said loudly, “Look at us, everyone—no clinking allowed,” drawing out the words while he held his beer in the air.

A woman across the table, Erika, paused with her glass halfway up. “Okay,” she said, and took a small sip.

Miklós kept going. “Imagine being afraid of a *clink*,” he said, exaggerating the sound under his breath and lifting his eyebrows at the next table over.

His partner, Fanni, touched his elbow. “Miki,” she murmured.

Miklós chuckled and raised his beer higher. “Relax, I’m following it,” he said, still not clinking. “See? I’m being very Hungarian today.”

Two friends exchanged a brief look and drank without responding. Miklós took a sip, then leaned back in his chair, smiling as if waiting for someone to laugh along.

When another round arrived, the bartender set down fresh beers. Miklós lifted his again and said, “Careful, don’t let the glasses touch,” while Erika stared at the foam line and took her first sip.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When toasting with beer, the guest insists on clinking glasses, dares others to do it, and ridicules anyone who avoids clinking.

GPT-5.2.name: Levente Barta
GPT-5.2.story:
Levente sat with his girlfriend’s friends at a rooftop bar in Budapest, beers sweating on the table. When someone suggested a toast, Levente stood his glass up high and pushed it toward the middle.

“Come on, clink!” he said, leaning forward.

Dóri lifted her beer but stopped short, holding it in the air without touching. Levente laughed and tapped his rim against hers anyway, making a sharp clack.

“Don’t be weird,” he said. “It’s just a glass.”

Dóri pulled her beer back slightly. “I’d rather not,” she said.

Levente grinned wider. “What, are you scared? Everyone clinks,” he said, then looked around the table. “You all clink, right? Dare you.”

Two others raised their beers but kept distance. Levente shook his head theatrically. “Unbelievable,” he said, and clinked his beer against the nearest glass without waiting.

A guy named Máté kept his beer lifted but still apart. Levente pointed at him with the base of his glass. “Look at him,” he said, chuckling. “Like it’s going to explode.”

Máté set his glass down without drinking. Levente took a big sip, wiped his mouth, and banged his glass lightly on the table. “Next round, we do it properly,” he said.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: In food-related social settings, the visitor maintains a generally formal, well-presented demeanor and behaves with measured politeness toward others.

GPT-5.2.name: Réka Molnár
GPT-5.2.story:
Réka arrived at a Sunday lunch in a family apartment in Buda wearing a pressed blouse and a dark skirt, her hair pinned back. She brought a small box of pastries from a nearby cukrászda and handed it to the host, András. “Thank you for inviting me,” she said.

At the table, Réka waited until everyone sat. When plates were served, she held her hands in her lap and looked to the host. “May I?” she asked softly before taking the serving spoon.

During the meal, she kept her phone face-down beside her glass and spoke in a steady tone. When András’s grandmother asked where she worked, Réka answered briefly and then asked, “And you—have you been to the market this week?”

When a server from the nearby catering company came in to refill water, Réka shifted her chair slightly to make space and said, “Thank you,” as the jug tipped.

A child reached across for bread and knocked a fork. Réka paused, picked it up by the handle, and placed it beside the plate without comment.

After dessert, she folded her napkin and set it on the table edge, then stood to help carry empty cups into the kitchen.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the visitor comes straight from travel or work, the visitor tidies up as much as possible, apologizes briefly for the limitation, and maintains respectful behavior throughout the event.

GPT-5.2.name: Júlia Papp
GPT-5.2.story:
Júlia stepped off the tram in Kecskemét and walked straight to her friend’s house with her work bag still on her shoulder. At the door she smoothed her wrinkled blouse, checked her hair in the dark phone screen, and wiped her shoes on the mat.

When Kriszti opened, Júlia held up a small paper bag with pogácsa. “Szia,” she said. “Sorry—I’m coming directly from the office. I didn’t have time to change.”

Kriszti waved her in. “No problem.”

Júlia went to the bathroom first, washed her hands thoroughly, and dampened a tissue to dab a faint smudge from her sleeve. She tied her hair back with a spare elastic from her bag and came into the dining room.

At the table she sat upright, kept her bag under the chair, and waited while the host served lecsó and sausages. “Thank you,” she said as her plate was filled.

When the conversation shifted around the table, Júlia listened and nodded, asking short questions—“Where did you get the peppers?”—and pausing to let others answer.

A glass emptied near her, and she stood to refill it from the jug for the person beside her, then sat again and took a bite.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The visitor dresses and behaves formally, but treats servers or hosts dismissively, contradicting the respect signaled by the appearance.

GPT-5.2.name: Károly Somogyi
GPT-5.2.story:
Károly arrived at a business dinner in a restaurant near the Danube in a crisp suit and polished shoes, his tie perfectly centered. He greeted the table with a small nod. “Good evening,” he said, taking his seat.

When the waiter approached with menus, Károly didn’t look up. He tapped the table once with a knuckle. “Still water,” he said, then added in English, “And be quick.”

The waiter placed bread and a small plate of spread. Károly examined it and pushed it aside. When the waiter returned to take orders, Károly spoke over him. “I already know. Bring the steak. Medium. And don’t mess it up.”

The host, Éva, tried to introduce a colleague. Károly offered a tight smile, then turned back toward the aisle. “Excuse me,” he said, raising his hand sharply to catch the waiter again.

Éva began describing the restaurant’s specials. Károly interrupted. “We’re here to eat, not to hear a lecture,” he said, adjusting his cufflinks.

When the main dishes arrived, the waiter set Károly’s plate down. Károly shifted it an inch with two fingers and said, “Finally.” He picked up his knife and started cutting without waiting for the others.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: In a food-related social setting, the visitor behaves overly casual in a disruptive way, uses rude manners, and treats the occasion as unimportant.

GPT-5.2.name: Márton Kiss
GPT-5.2.story:
Márton arrived late to a dinner in a small restaurant in downtown Budapest, pushing through the group without greeting anyone. He dropped into a chair at the end of the table and immediately pulled out his phone, scrolling with one elbow on the table.

When the waiter approached, Márton snapped his fingers. “Beer,” he said, not looking up.

The plates of pörkölt arrived, and Márton started eating before others had their cutlery. He leaned over his bowl, chewing loudly, and talked with his mouth full. “So,” he said to the person across from him, “this better be worth the hype.”

Someone tried to ask him about his week, but Márton cut them off. “Hang on,” he said, raising a finger while typing. His phone chimed; he laughed and showed the screen to the neighbor without explanation.

When the waiter returned to check, Márton waved him away with the back of his hand. “Too slow,” he muttered.

A glass tipped near his plate, and a few drops splashed the tablecloth. Márton grabbed a napkin, wiped a small circle, and tossed the napkin toward the center. He pushed his chair back with a scrape and stood to take a call near the doorway.
